 {"id":"bd-pwz","title":"Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync","description":"# Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync

## Goal
Bridge asupersync HTTP streaming response to the existing SSE parser (src/sse.rs).

## Background
The SSE parser in src/sse.rs is already complete and well-tested:
- SseParser::new() creates parser
- SseParser::feed(&str) returns Vec<SseEvent>
- Handles: event types, data fields, IDs, comments, multiline data
- 9 unit tests covering all edge cases

The challenge is connecting asupersync's streaming response body to this parser.

## Implementation

### Streaming Adapter
```rust
// src/http/sse.rs (new file or extend existing)

use crate::sse::{SseParser, SseEvent};
use asupersync::Cx;

pub struct SseStream {
    parser: SseParser,
    body: BodyReader,
    buffer: String,
}

impl SseStream {
    pub fn new(response: Response) -> Self {
        Self {
            parser: SseParser::new(),
            body: response.into_body(),
            buffer: String::new(),
        }
    }
    
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Option<Result<SseEvent, Error>> {
        loop {
            // Try to parse buffered data first
            let events = self.parser.feed(&self.buffer);
            if !events.is_empty() {
                self.buffer.clear();
                return Some(Ok(events.remove(0)));
            }
            
            // Read more data from body stream
            match self.body.read_chunk(cx).await {
                Ok(Some(chunk)) => {
                    self.buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
                }
                Ok(None) => return None,  // Stream ended
                Err(e) => return Some(Err(e.into())),
            }
        }
    }
}

// Or implement as a proper Stream trait for use with async iteration
impl Stream for SseStream {
    type Item = Result<SseEvent, Error>;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<Self::Item>> {
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Usage Pattern (in providers)
```rust
// In anthropic.rs
let response = client.post(url).json(&request).send(cx).await?;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);

while let Some(event) = sse.next(cx).await {
    match event? {
        SseEvent { data, event_type, .. } => {
            // Process Anthropic streaming event
        }
    }
}
```

## Considerations
1. **Backpressure**: Don't buffer entire response; process incrementally
2. **Error recovery**: Handle malformed events gracefully (log and continue)
3. **Cancellation**: Support clean shutdown via Cx cancellation
4. **Memory**: Limit buffer size to prevent OOM on malicious servers

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper must exist first)

## Testing
- Unit test: SseStream parses single event
- Unit test: SseStream handles multiline data
- Unit test: SseStream handles stream interruption
- Integration test: Stream from mock HTTP server

## Files
- src/http/sse.rs (new or extend)
- src/http/mod.rs (add re-export)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SseStream wraps Response and yields SseEvent
- [ ] Incremental parsing works (no full buffering)
- [ ] Cancellation handled cleanly
- [ ] Memory bounded
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CodexGPT52","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:22.790834667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:56.891021395Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:12:15.003659099Z","close_reason":"Completed (SSE parsing + streaming adapter in src/sse.rs; http::sse re-exports; tests added; all quality gates pass)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pwz","depends_on_id":"bd-9sa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-pwz","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-pwz","author":"CodexGPT52","text":"Claimed by CodexGPT52. Plan: (1) remove duplicated SSE impl in src/http/sse.rs by re-exporting the canonical crate::sse::{SseEvent,SseParser,SseStream} (better UTF-8 chunk handling + avoids tech debt), (2) add focused unit tests for SseStream wrapper behavior (UTF-8 split + multi-event chunks), (3) run cargo check/clippy/fmt/test, then close bd-pwz. Will coordinate with bd-9sa owner if any gating fixes are needed.","created_at":"2026-02-03T16:57:53Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qpm","title":"Apply theme colors to TUI components","description":"# Apply theme colors to TUI components

## Goal
Wire theme colors into lipgloss styles and glamour markdown rendering.

## Implementation

### Create Themed Styles
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn create_styles(theme: &Theme) -> Styles {
    Styles {
        message_user: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.accent)),
        
        message_assistant: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.foreground)),
        
        thinking: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.muted))
            .italic(true),
        
        error: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.error))
            .bold(true),
        
        success: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.success)),
        
        border: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .border_foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.ui.border)),
        
        // ... more styles
    }
}
```

### Apply to PiApp
```rust
impl PiApp {
    pub fn new(config: Config) -> Self {
        let theme = config.theme()
            .map(|name| Theme::load_by_name(&name).ok())
            .flatten()
            .unwrap_or_else(Theme::dark);
        
        let styles = create_styles(&theme);
        
        Self {
            theme,
            styles,
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Apply to Glamour (Markdown)
```rust
fn create_glamour_style(theme: &Theme) -> glamour::Style {
    glamour::Style {
        document: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.foreground.clone()),
            background_color: Some(theme.colors.background.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        heading: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.accent.clone()),
            bold: Some(true),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        code: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.syntax.string.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        // ... more elements
    }
}
```

## Components to Update
1. Message display (user/assistant)
2. Thinking block
3. Status line
4. Input field border
5. Error messages
6. Tool status
7. Markdown rendering
8. Code blocks

## Dependencies
- bd-37a (theme loader must exist)

## Testing
- Test: Styles created from theme
- Test: Default theme applies
- Test: Custom theme applies

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All TUI components use theme colors
- [ ] Glamour uses syntax colors
- [ ] Default theme looks good
- [ ] Custom themes render correctly","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

-{"id":"bd-qudx1","title":"[SEC-5.1] Security control center and real-time runtime alerts with reason codes","description":"## Background
Users need immediate context when an extension action is blocked, hardened, or terminated.

## Scope
- Build runtime security event views (interactive + RPC) showing score, reason codes, and policy source.
- Provide actionable remediation suggestions and safe override pathway.
- Support filtering by extension/session/time window.

## Deliverables
- Security event UI surfaces and structured RPC events.
- Alert taxonomy tied to enforcement actions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Alerts include who/what/why/action fields.
- [ ] Users can distinguish policy denial from anomaly denial quickly.
- [ ] Alert stream is stable for downstream integrations.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusAgent","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:42.116937618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T11:07:17.435754610Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T11:07:17.435663740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","security","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qudx1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vbax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:01.180554881Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qudx1","depends_on_id":"bd-3i9da","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.929262663Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qudx1","depends_on_id":"bd-3tb30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.701612568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1427,"issue_id":"bd-qudx1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon continuing bd-qudx1 (SEC-5.1). All dependencies now closed (SEC-3.4, SEC-4.4, SEC-3.5). Will implement: (1) SecurityAlert struct with who/what/why/action fields, (2) alert taxonomy mapped to enforcement actions, (3) structured RPC event format for downstream integrations.","created_at":"2026-02-14T10:51:42Z"},{"id":1429,"issue_id":"bd-qudx1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification note from RainyMill: All 3 acceptance criteria are already satisfied by existing implementation: (1) SecurityAlert struct has who(extension_id)/what(category,capability,method)/why(reason_codes,summary,policy_source)/action(action,remediation) fields; (2) SecurityAlertCategory enum distinguishes PolicyDenial from AnomalyDenial with separate counters in SecurityAlertCategoryCounts; (3) SecurityAlertArtifact with schema 'pi.ext.security_alert.v1' provides stable export format. record_security_alert() and security_alert_artifact() on ExtensionManager are implemented. Code compiles clean.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:00:58Z"},{"id":1432,"issue_id":"bd-qudx1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-5.1 complete. Implemented:

1. **SecurityAlert factory methods** (AC1 - who/what/why/action):
   - `from_policy_denial()` - capability policy blocks
   - `from_exec_mediation()` - dangerous command blocks (with/without class label)
   - `from_secret_redaction()` - secret broker redactions
   - `from_anomaly_detection()` - risk scorer anomaly decisions (auto-severity from action)
   - `from_quarantine()` - extension termination
   - `from_enforcement_transition()` - enforcement state machine changes

2. **Alert taxonomy** (AC2 - policy vs anomaly distinction):
   - SecurityAlertCategory: PolicyDenial, AnomalyDenial, ExecMediation, SecretBroker, QuotaBreach, Quarantine, ProfileTransition
   - SecurityAlertSeverity: Info, Warning, Error, Critical (with Ord for filtering)
   - SecurityAlertAction: Allow, Harden, Prompt, Deny, Terminate, Redact

3. **Stable alert stream** (AC3 - downstream integrations):
   - emit_security_alert() records + traces at appropriate level (error/warn/info/debug)
   - query_security_alerts() with SecurityAlertFilter (category, min_severity, extension_id, after_ts_ms)
   - SecurityAlert has schema version tag and sequence_id for stable deserialization
   - Full serde roundtrip support

**Tests**: 21 new tests. Total 491 extensions tests pass. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:07:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] cargo build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Binary size reduced","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

 {"id":"bd-rhyl","title":"Define scoring rubric + weights","description":"# Goal
Create a transparent scoring model to rank extensions fairly and consistently.

# Deliverables
- Weighted criteria: popularity (downloads/stars), recency, maintenance activity, license permissiveness, runtime compatibility, reliability risk.
- Clear normalization rules and tie‑breakers.
- Documented rationale for each weight.

# Notes
This rubric must be simple enough to apply manually yet defensible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:16.187391961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:18.858409964Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:18.858321008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:16.202684254Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","depends_on_id":"bd-97qh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.901909784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":331,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Popularity alone is not enough; we need a balanced evaluation across usability and maintainability.

Reasoning: A weighted rubric makes selection reproducible and defensible.

Considerations: Keep the rubric simple, transparent, and aligned with Pi runtime constraints.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:37Z"},{"id":381,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"MAKE THIS EXECUTABLE + TESTED

To avoid “hand-wavy” selection, implement the rubric as a deterministic scoring function and test it.

Requirements
- Score breakdown per criterion + total score.
- Explicit handling of missing data (unknown != 0).
- Marketplace metrics support (blocked on bd-97qh).

Tests
- Unit tests for normalization + tie-breakers.
- Golden examples (3+) asserted exactly.
- Offline E2E: run scoring over a fixture candidate pool and emit a stable ranked list.

Logging
- Emit a run summary (histograms + top-N lists + any manual overrides flagged).
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:11:51Z"},{"id":587,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scoring rubric fully defined and implemented: EXTENSION_POPULARITY_CRITERIA.md has weighted criteria (Popularity 30, Adoption 15, Coverage 20, Activity 15, Compatibility 20) with normalization rules, tie-breakers (Coverage > Popularity > Recency > ID), and risk penalty (0-15). extension_scoring.rs implements all scoring functions deterministically. ext_score_candidates binary produces ranked JSON output. 60+ unit tests verify all thresholds, tie-breakers, and edge cases. 3 worked examples in docs.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s1hx","title":"Task: Implement input event (transforming, blocking)","description":"# Task: Implement Input Event (Transforming, Blocking)

## Objective

Dispatch the input event before processing user messages, allowing extensions to transform or block input.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers('input')) {
    const inputResult = await runner.emit({
        type: 'input',
        content: userMessage.content,
        attachments: userMessage.attachments,
    });
    
    if (inputResult?.block) {
        console.log(inputResult.reason || 'Input blocked');
        return;
    }
    
    if (inputResult?.content) {
        userMessage.content = inputResult.content;
    }
}
```

## Event Details

### input Event
- **When**: Before processing user message
- **Purpose**: Transform, filter, or block input
- **Data**: content, attachments

### Return Value
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize)]
pub struct InputEventResult {
    pub content: Option<String>,      // Transformed content
    pub block: bool,                   // If true, block processing
    pub reason: Option<String>,        // Reason for blocking
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_user_input(
        &mut self,
        content: String,
        attachments: Vec<Attachment>,
    ) -> Result<Option<UserMessage>> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("input") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::Input {
                    content: content.clone(),
                    attachments: attachments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<InputEventResult>(event).await? {
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(|| "Input blocked".to_string());
                        self.display_message(&reason)?;
                        return Ok(None);
                    }
                    
                    let final_content = result.content.unwrap_or(content);
                    return Ok(Some(UserMessage { content: final_content, attachments }));
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Some(UserMessage { content, attachments }))
    }
}
```

## Use Cases

1. **Macro Expansion**: Replace !date with current date
2. **Content Filter**: Block PII
3. **Translation**: Auto-translate input
4. **Logging**: Record all user inputs

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (5 cases)
1. test_no_handlers_uses_original
2. test_handler_returns_null_uses_original
3. test_handler_transforms_content
4. test_handler_blocks_input
5. test_block_reason_displayed

### E2E Test Script
Extension with macro expansion and content filter, JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] input event dispatched before processing
- [ ] Transformation applied correctly
- [ ] Blocking works with reason display
- [ ] 5 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: Implemented input event transform/block handling in interactive + AgentSession (apply_input_event_response helper, UpdateLastUserMessage for UI), removed duplicate input dispatch, and ensured before_agent_start dispatch still runs. Fixed clippy/test warnings in tests/provider_factory.rs, tests/agent_loop_vcr.rs, tests/e2e_cli.rs, tests/compaction.rs.
